methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Strata Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Eric Zhou and Alex Mason, having raised $5.2M to date. The comparable universe for robotics and manufacturing includes one public company and eight private peers, though narrowly-filtered same-stage and adjacent-stage cohorts remain sparse.

Valuation analysis relies primarily on stage-progression methodology, as peer-median comparison cannot be reliably applied. The comparable set in Robotics & Manufacturing (Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and others) lacks sufficient concentration at equivalent stages or valuation tiers to support statistical benchmarking. Using total capital raised as a proxy base of $5.2M and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next financing round yields an implied valuation of $13M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $15M, with a median point of $13M. This range reflects conservative application of stage-progression mechanics and acknowledges material uncertainty around comparable company selection. Key risks to valuation include the thinness of narrow peer data—which limits triangulation to a single method—and the inherent variability in step-up multiples across market cycles and sub-sector dynamics within robotics. Investors should weight this valuation accordingly pending access to deeper comparable company metrics or more granular revenue and growth trajectory data.
